message.content:
Mechanical line-relocation in each file below. Each currently has a line `source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buym_yelp.sh"` INDENTED inside a furnish function (e.g. `zXXX_furnish()`). It must move to the file HEADER.

EXACT transformation per file:
1. DELETE the existing line that reads (with its leading whitespace):  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buym_yelp.sh"
   — the one inside the furnish function. Remove the whole line.
2. INSERT a new line `source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buym_yelp.sh"` (NO leading whitespace) immediately AFTER the header line that reads exactly:  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
   (that buc_command line is near the top of the file, at file scope / not inside any function).

Net effect: the buym_yelp.sh source moves from inside furnish to the header, directly below the buc_command.sh source. Change nothing else. Every one of these files uses ${BURD_BUK_DIR} for both lines, so the variable is unchanged.

Verify after editing each file: `grep -n 'buc_command.sh\|buym_yelp.sh' <file>` should show the two on consecutive lines near the top, and NO buym_yelp.sh line remaining inside the furnish function.

attachment.content:
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the current diff and apply the fixes — equivalent to /code-review --fix.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# RBGV CLI - Recipe Bottle Access Probe command-line interface
20	#
21	# Surfaces the credential access probes implemented by the rbgv library
22	# module as four operator tabtargets (one per role). Theurge consumes these
23	# as plain subprocesses, owning no colophons of its own.
24	#
25	# Commands:
26	#   check_governor   JWT SA access probe for the governor credential
27	#   check_retriever  JWT SA access probe for the retriever credential
28	#   check_director   JWT SA access probe for the director credential
29	#   check_payor      OAuth access probe for the payor credential
30	
31	set -euo pipefail
32	
33	source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
34	
35	######################################################################
36	# CLI Commands
37	
38	# JWT SA propagation-absorbing budget: 60 attempts × 3000ms ≈ 180s worst case.
39	# Happy path exits on first 2xx; budget consumed only when post-mint IAM
40	# propagation lags. Each retry mints a fresh JWT-OAuth token, so the inner
41	# rbgo_get_token_capture race (BBAAp) is exercised independently per attempt.
42	zrbgv_jwt_check() {
43	  local -r z_role="$1"
44	  local -r z_iterations=60
45	  local -r z_delay_ms=3000
46	  buc_step "JWT SA access probe: ${z_role}"
47	  rbgv_jwt_sa_probe "${z_role}" "${z_iterations}" "${z_delay_ms}"
48	  buc_success "${z_role} JWT access probe passed"
49	}
50	
51	rbgv_check_governor() {
52	  zrbgv_sentinel
53	  buc_doc_brief "Check the governor credential reaches Google Cloud (JWT SA access probe)"
54	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
55	  zrbgv_jwt_check "governor"
56	}
57	
58	rbgv_check_retriever() {
59	  zrbgv_sentinel
60	  buc_doc_brief "Check the retriever credential reaches Google Cloud (JWT SA access probe)"
61	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
62	  zrbgv_jwt_check "retriever"
63	}
64	
65	rbgv_check_director() {
66	  zrbgv_sentinel
67	  buc_doc_brief "Check the director credential reaches Google Cloud (JWT SA access probe)"
68	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
69	  zrbgv_jwt_check "director"
70	}
71	
72	rbgv_check_payor() {
73	  zrbgv_sentinel
74	  buc_doc_brief "Check the payor credential reaches Google Cloud (OAuth access probe)"
75	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
76	
77	  # Payor probe semantic: stability-sample loop.
78	  local -r z_iterations=5
79	  local -r z_delay_ms=1500
80	  buc_step "Payor OAuth access probe"
81	  source "${RBCC_rbrp_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source RBRP: ${RBCC_rbrp_file}"
82	  zrbrp_kindle
83	  zrbrp_enforce
84	  rbgv_payor_oauth_probe "${z_iterations}" "${z_delay_ms}"
85	  buc_success "Payor OAuth access probe passed"
86	}
87

138	  zrbgv_kindle

37	rbob_validate() {

39	
40	  buc_doc_brief "Validate that RBOB configuration is complete and runnable"
41	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
42	
43	  buc_step "RBOB Validate: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
44	
45	  # All values computed at kindle - just verify they exist
46	  test -n "${ZRBOB_RUNTIME}" || buc_die "ZRBOB_RUNTIME not set"
47	  test -n "${ZRBOB_SENTRY}" || buc_die "ZRBOB_SENTRY not set"

49	  test -n "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}" || buc_die "ZRBOB_BOTTLE not set"

51	  test -f "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_SCRIPT}" || buc_die "Sentry script not found: ${ZRBOB_SENTRY_SCRIPT}"

57	  echo "Runtime:       ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME}"
58	  echo "Sentry:        ${ZRBOB_SENTRY}"
59	  echo "Pentacle:        ${ZRBOB_PENTACLE}"
60	  echo "Bottle:        ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}"
61	  echo "Network:       ${ZRBOB_NETWORK}"
62	  echo "Compose base:  ${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}"
63	  echo "Compose frag:  ${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_FRAGMENT} ($(test -f "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_FRAGMENT}" && echo 'exists' || echo 'not found'))"
64	}

72	  buc_step "RBOB Info: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
73	  echo "Runtime:   ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME}"
74	  echo "Sentry:    ${ZRBOB_SENTRY}"

108	  buc_doc_env_done || return 0
109	
110	  local z_command="${1:-}"
111	
112	  local z_rbk_kit_dir="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
113	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buym_yelp.sh"
114	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
115	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
116	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrn_regime.sh"
117	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbcc_Constants.sh"
118	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrr_regime.sh"
119	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrd_regime.sh"
120	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh"
121	  source "${RBCC_rbrr_file}"
122	  source "${RBCC_rbrd_file}"
123	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgc_Constants.sh"
124	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgl_GarLayout.sh"
125	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgd_DepotConstants.sh"
126	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgo_OAuth.sh"
127	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbob_bottle.sh"
128	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buf_fact.sh"
129	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh"
130	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh"
131	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbfk_kludge.sh"
132	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rboo_observe.sh"
133	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buz_zipper.sh"
134	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbz_zipper.sh"
135	
136	  zbuv_kindle
137	  zburd_kindle
138	  zrbcc_kindle
139	
140	  local z_folio="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
141	  if test -z "${z_folio}"; then
142	    local z_monikers
143	    z_monikers=$(rbrn_list_capture) || buc_die "No nameplates found"
144	    buc_step "Available nameplates:"
145	    local z_moniker=""
146	    for z_moniker in ${z_monikers}; do
147	      buc_bare "        ${z_moniker}"
148	    done
149	    buc_die "Nameplate moniker required (pass as argument)"
150	  fi
151	  local z_nameplate_file="${RBCC_moorings_dir}/${z_folio}/${RBCC_rbrn_file}"
152	  test -f "${z_nameplate_file}" || buc_die "Nameplate not found: ${z_nameplate_file}"
153	  source "${z_nameplate_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source nameplate: ${z_nameplate_file}"
154	  zrbrn_kindle
155	
156	  # Differential enforce: nameplate-keyed kludge commands write
157	  # RBRN_*_HALLMARK, so zrbrn_enforce would self-veto on marshal-zero
158	  # state where those fields are blank by design (rblm zeroes them;
159	  # rbtdrp_pristine treats them as RBTDRP_RBRN_BLANK_FIELDS). Strict
160	  # validation lives in rbw-rnv (rbrn_cli.sh). Mirrors the yoke/RBRV
161	  # split (rbfl_cli vs rbrv_cli). rbob_ordain (rbw-tO) keeps enforce —
162	  # it is the nameplate-keyed gate before incurring Cloud Build spend;
163	  # the foundry-keyed ordain (rbw-fO → rbfd_ordain) is vessel-only and
164	  # never enters this CLI, so onboarding-sequence's ordain steps are
165	  # unaffected.
166	  case "${z_command}" in
167	    rbob_kludge|rbob_kludge_bottle|rbob_kludge_sentry)
168	      ;;
169	    *)
170	      zrbrn_enforce
171	      ;;
172	  esac
173	
174	  zrbrr_kindle
175	  zrbrd_kindle
176	  zrbrr_enforce
177	  zrbrd_enforce
178	  zrbdc_kindle
179	  zrbgc_kindle
180	  zrbgl_kindle
181	  zrbgd_kindle
182	  zrbgo_kindle
183	  zrbob_kindle
184	
185	  # rbfh kindle (Dockerfile hygiene) — load-bearing for both kludge and ordain
186	  # since both wired callsites invoke rbfh_dockerfile_check.
187	  zrbfh_kindle
188	
189	  # Differential kindle: kludge uses uncredentialed rbfk; ordain needs full rbfd
190	  case "${z_command}" in
191	    rbob_kludge|rbob_kludge_bottle|rbob_kludge_sentry)
192	      zrbfk_kindle
193	      ;;
194	    rbob_ordain)
195	      zrbfd_kindle
196	      ;;
197	  esac
198	
199	  zbuz_kindle
200	  zrbz_kindle
201	}
202	
203	buc_execute rbob_ "Recipe Bottle Orchestration" zrbob_furnish "$@"

45	  printf "${z_row_fmt}" \

57	
58	      local z_sentry_local="--"
59	      local z_bottle_local="--"

63	      else
64	        z_sentry_local="??"
65	        z_bottle_local="??"
66	      fi

68	      local z_ws_port="${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION:-}"
69	      local z_enc_port="${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE:-}"

18	#
19	# RBFH CLI - Recipe Bottle Foundry Hygiene command-line interface
20	#
21	# Surfaces the Dockerfile FROM-line constraint enforced by the rbfh
22	# library module so that theurge fixtures (and operators) can drive
23	# the contract from outside the kludge/conjure code paths.
24	#
25	# Commands:
26	#   check         Check a Dockerfile path for FROM-line hygiene
27	#   check_vessel  Check a vessel's RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE for hygiene
28	
29	set -euo pipefail
30	
31	source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
32	
33	######################################################################
34	# CLI Commands
35	
36	rbfh_check() {
37	  zrbfh_sentinel
38	
39	  buc_doc_brief "Check a Dockerfile against the FROM-line hygiene contract"
40	  buc_doc_param "dockerfile" "Path to the Dockerfile to check"
41	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
42	
43	  local -r z_path="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
44	  test -n "${z_path}" || buc_die "Dockerfile path required (free-form path argument)"
45	
46	  buc_step "RBFH Check: ${z_path}"
47	  rbfh_dockerfile_check "${z_path}"
48	  buc_success "Dockerfile passes hygiene contract: ${z_path}"
49	}
50	
51	rbfh_check_vessel() {
52	  zrbfh_sentinel
53	  zrbfc_sentinel
54	
55	  buc_doc_brief "Check a vessel's conjure Dockerfile against the FROM-line hygiene contract"
56	  buc_doc_param "vessel" "Vessel sigil or path to vessel directory"
57	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
58	
59	  # Resolve vessel argument (sigil or path) — lists-and-dies on missing/invalid
60	  zrbfc_resolve_vessel "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
61	  local -r z_vessel_dir=$(<"${ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE}")
62	  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Empty resolved vessel path"
63	
64	  # Source the vessel's rbrv.env to pick up RBRV_VESSEL_MODE and RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE
65	  source "${z_vessel_dir}/rbrv.env" || buc_die "Failed to source vessel rbrv.env: ${z_vessel_dir}/rbrv.env"
66	
67	  # Hygiene is a property of the FROM line; non-conjure vessels have no
68	  # local Dockerfile, so the contract is vacuously satisfied. Exit silently
69	  # so callers iterating the whole fleet need not pre-filter by mode.
70	  if test "${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE:-}" != "rbnve_conjure"; then
71	    buc_info "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL:-${z_vessel_dir}}' is mode '${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE:-<unset>}' — no Dockerfile to check"
72	    return 0
73	  fi
74	
75	  test -n "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE:-}" \
76	    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL:-${z_vessel_dir}}' has no RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE"
77	
78	  buc_step "RBFH Check Vessel: ${RBRV_SIGIL:-${z_vessel_dir}} → ${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}"
79	  rbfh_dockerfile_check "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}"
80	  buc_success "Vessel Dockerfile passes hygiene contract: ${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}"
81	}
82
